PONG2 Training: Building Custom KIR Prediction Models

Norman Lab

Source: vignettes/PONG2-training.Rmd
PONG2-training.Rmd

Overview

This vignette provides a complete, step-by-step guide to training custom KIR prediction models using the train command in PONG2. Training is useful when:

-You have a new or population-specific reference dataset -You want to update models with additional samples -You need locus-specific models (e.g. only KIR3DL1/S1) -You want to experiment with different SNP quality filters

Prerequisites:

  • PLINK 2.0 installed and in PATH
  • R ≥ 4.0 with PONG2 installed
  • Reference PLINK files (chr19 or KIR region)
  • Known KIR allele calls in CSV format

Input Data Requirements

  1. Reference genotypes (–bfile)
  • PLINK bed/bim/fam files containing SNPs in the KIR locus (chr19)
  • Recommended: extract region first to reduce size
plink2 \
  --bfile full_reference \
  --chr 19 \
  --make-bed \
  --out reference_chr19
  1. Known KIR calls (–kfile)

CSV file with sample IDs and allele calls Minimal format with header optional:

sample_id KIR2DL1_h1 KIR2DL1_h2 KIR3DL1_h1 KIR3DL1_h2
SAMPLE001 *00101 *00302 *00101 *00201
SAMPLE002 *00104 *0000 *00401 *00501
SAMPLE003 *009 *00601 *00302 *00101
  • Columns: two per KIR locus/gene where h1 and h2 represent KIR haplotypes
  • Alleles: use standard nomenclature (e.g. 001, 00201)
  • null allele: leave as *0000

Run training 

pong2 train \
  -i reference_chr19 \
  -k kir_calls.csv \
  -o models/custom \
  -l KIR \
  -a hg38 \
  -t 10

After training completes, the output directory (-o) will contain:

  • model.rds or model.RData — trained prediction model (main file)
  • training_log.txt — summary of training process, metrics, and warnings
  • tmp/ — temporary files (can be deleted)

Using a custom model for imputation 

pong2 impute \
  -i chr19 \
  -o results/ \
  -l KIR \
  -a hg38 \
  -m models/custom/model.rds

Note: The -m, --model flag is used to specify a custom model file.

Troubleshooting Training

  • “No matching samples” → Ensure sample IDs in –kfile exactly match PLINK .fam
  • Low SNP coverage → Increase –filter or pre-impute reference panel
  • Memory issues → Reduce threads or use a machine with more RAM
  • Slow training → Use more –threads or smaller reference dataset
  • See vignette PONG2-imputation for detailed imputation workflow
  • See vignette PONG2-training for custom model training
  • Report issues: GitHub Issues

On this page

For more details on model structure and prediction logic, see the package reference or source code. Questions or issues? Open a GitHub issue. Happy model training! 🧬

Developed by Suraju A. Sadeeq, Laura A. Leaton, Paul Norman.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
